Aggregated bias · 1-glance synthesis
Today's verdict NEUTRAL-CAUTIOUS
When the signals disagree · and which one to trust
Different gauges sometimes point opposite ways. That is normal. Here is each clash in plain words, with the side to lean on for now.
⚠ These two disagree: Crash Index **3/10** WARNING with VIX backwardation and skew both active vs Regime state STABLE at score **16**, bias QUIET, zero confluences active
Options markets are paying up for near-dated protection while the slow-moving Regime diffusion index shows nothing. Trust the vol side. Backwardation is real cash bid for hedges; the Regime score lags by construction.
⚠ These two disagree: KOSPI **-9.7%** (5d) and NDX **-3.4%** (5d) vs Zero active confluences and Regime STABLE
A **-9.7%** five-day print in a major benchmark is already a crash-tier move. The confluence framework requires specific triple-alignments (VIX backwardation with credit, small-caps, or JPY) and it has not caught up. Trust the tape.
⚠ These two disagree: Divergence composite ELEVATED at **51.2%** diverging with mother-jaws at **100** vs Zero confluences firing, macro regime BROADENING at only **30%** confidence
Divergence measures correlation breakage directly across **43** pairs and it is at the ceiling. Confluences and regime call are slower composites. Trust divergence for early warning; use confluence for the confirming trigger.
Today's tension
Surface calm, internals cracking: the price tape has not confirmed what the vol market and cross-asset correlations are already pricing.
Bull
The Regime score prints **16** (STABLE), zero of three confluences are firing, and BTC is flat at **-0.6%** on five days. HSI leads Asia at **+2.0%** and DAX leads Europe at **+1.9%**, so global risk-on holds outside a few pockets.
Bear
The Crash Index sits at **3/10** WARNING, the VIX Spike Detector reads Tier **2/4** MEDIUM at **60%** probability with backwardation and skew both active, and the divergence composite is ELEVATED with **51.2%** of pairs breaking (**17** bearish vs **5** bullish across **43** pairs). Kospi is down **-9.7%** in five days, Nasdaq down **-3.4%**.
The tell: Watch the VIX term structure. If it exits backwardation while NDX defends the **-3.4%** week, the crash bid gets faded. If NDX breaks lower and pulls one confluence live, the vol market was early and correct.
AI read · Citrini · today's macro tape

The tape looks calm, the plumbing does not. Crash Index at 3/10 WARNING has three surveillance signals lit, the VIX Spike Detector reads Tier 2/4 MEDIUM with 60% probability, and both backwardation (near-dated vol bid above longer-dated) and skew (puts bid over calls) are hot. Meanwhile the Regime score is 16 (STABLE), the confluence count is 0 of 3, and cross-asset relationships are fracturing with 51.2% of pairs diverging (17 bearish, 5 bullish). Global leadership splits ugly: HSI +2.0% and DAX +1.9% hold up, while KOSPI -9.7% and NDX -3.4% bleed under a Dow that is flat at +0.1%.

Positioning follows the rotation, not the panic. You lean lighter on cap-weighted tech and long-duration equity because the vol market is bidding downside there. NDX -3.4% against DJI +0.1% on the same week is a clean defensives-over-tech rotation happening inside a quiet index surface, which lines up with the Contrarian score at 45 (ACCUMULATE, partial) and the macro regime read of BROADENING at 30% confidence. Broadening favors laggards and equal-weight over the mega-cap leaders, so you rotate rather than reduce gross. The KOSPI -9.7% print is your reminder that individual legs can snap without warning when the composite divergence is at ELEVATED.

Risk is the KOSPI trade getting imported. The bear case confirms if backwardation deepens, one of the three confluences lights up (credit, small-cap, or JPY), and NDX takes out its -3.4% low. The stance flips constructive if the VIX term structure normalizes out of backwardation and the divergence composite drops off ELEVATED with the Regime score holding at 16.

5-regime heuristic classifier · cross-asset
Macro Regime — where are we structurally
AI read · macro regime

The macro regime reads BROADENING at 30% confidence. That is a low-conviction call for a real theme: leadership widening away from the largest cap-weighted names.

The read lines up with the divergence tell of spy_rsp correlation flipping to +0.84 and the cross-region split of DJI +0.1% against NDX -3.4%. You position for equal-weight over cap-weight, laggard sectors over top-heavy leaders, and cyclicals with the copper-gold coupling at +0.86 still intact.

Confidence at only 30% means you size the rotation modestly and let either a confluence light up or the Contrarian score push past its current 45 before you commit more capital.

SPY above its 200-DMA with contained vol. This is the zone where long convexity compounds: leverage and call buying carry the lowest variance drag. Size up your highest-conviction directional calls here.
Gate from Leverage for the Long Run (Gayed/Bilello 2016): the durable edge is the 200-DMA × vol regime, the 3× compounding is marketing. Attention gauge for call sizing, not a trade trigger.

Dow Theory generalized · cross-asset divergences
Cross-asset divergence radar — Cross-asset divergence watch
AI read · divergences in plain English

The composite divergence sits ELEVATED at 51.2% of pairs breaking, with a net bearish tilt of +12 (17 bearish vs 5 bullish across 43 pairs) and the mother-jaws score pinned at 100. "Divergence" here means asset pairs that normally move together have stopped doing so.

"Correlation drift" is the change in their co-movement from an earlier window to the current one. The specific drifts tell the story.

spy_rsp flipped from -0.28 to +0.84, which says equal-weight has collapsed into cap-weight and leadership is narrowing. lqd_ief moved from -0.03 to +0.88, so investment-grade credit is now a pure rate trade.

t10y2y shifted from +0.62 to -0.60, a full reversal in how the curve rides its partner. hg_gc ran from -0.01 to +0.86, so copper and gold trade as one block now, which is the classic "reflation plus hedge at the same time" tell of a market that cannot decide between growth and fear.

Cross-asset confluence · live status
Confluences — most proven alpha of the framework 0/3 ACTIVE
AI read · what the confluences mean now

Zero of three confluences are firing. No VIX backwardation with credit stress, no VIX backwardation with small-cap drawdown, no VIX backwardation with yen safe-haven flow.

The vol market is nervous alone. HYG is not confirming, small caps are not confirming, JPY is not confirming.

That contains the fear to the equity vol surface and keeps it from spreading into the bigger macro channels. You do not press the short at this level.

You wait for at least one channel to light up before treating the crash bid as a real cross-asset event. Until then, the setup stays a rotation and hedge story, with the first confluence to fire as your green light to escalate.

✅
T1 × HYG (VIX backwardation + Credit stress)
Volatility backwardation meeting credit stress — the highest-conviction pre-correction setup in the framework. Fires only when options fear and the bond market confirm each other.

🌍
Asia · Europe · US · Crypto 24/7 — lead-lag detection
Cross-Region Surveillance
Major indices from the 4 regions/asset classes. Asia trades ahead time-wise vs US — a Tokyo overnight crash often leaks into the SPX open. Crypto = 24h leading indicator of global risk appetite.
AI read · the cross-region tape

The 24/7 tape splits four ways. Asia is a dispersion problem: HSI +2.0% leads while KOSPI -9.7% collapses, so Asian risk-on has one strong leg and one broken one.

Europe is quiet with DAX +1.9% leader and STOXX50E -0.1% laggard, flat overall. The US is where the rotation lives: DJI +0.1% against NDX -3.4% is a clean tech-out, defensives-in signal on the week.

Crypto is soft, BTC -0.6% and SOL -5.6%, which fits a state where vol is rising but no confluence has confirmed yet: the highest-beta risk asset bleeds first at the edges. The lead-lag says fade tech strength into the US open, watch the DJI-NDX spread to close via Nasdaq bounce or Dow give-back, and treat the KOSPI -9.7% as the outlier that has not been imported.

If it gets imported, small caps and cyclicals lose the rotation trade too.

Forward-looking indicators · Bonferroni-validated
Crash Index — 10 lead-signal radar 3/10 · WARNING — 3+ signals, active surveillance
AI read · volatility & crash, reconciled

The Crash Index at 3/10 WARNING and the VIX Spike Detector at Tier 2/4 MEDIUM (60%) are telling the same story from two angles. Price action stays out of crash mode and remains in surveillance: three signals are firing but no cascade.

The options market is meanwhile pricing near-term vol expansion via backwardation (near-dated variance above far-dated) and elevated skew (puts bid over calls). When the spot index stays put and vol goes bid, someone with capital is paying up for insurance and the price tape has not yet caught what hedgers already see.

Watch which side blinks first. If backwardation collapses without a spot drop, the crash bid was overreaction and you fade the puts.

If NDX confirms lower under the -3.4% base, the vol market was early and you cover shorts into weakness.

Plain-English read · the Crash Index
Think of this as a smoke alarm, not a market forecast. Each of the 10 rows is a signal that led real crashes, checked over 15 years of S&P 500 history (Bonferroni-validated, so it survived a strict false-positive test). In a calm market it sits near zero by design — a low number is the alarm working, not a broken gauge. It only earns your attention when 3 or more rows light up together. Today it reads 3/10 — a few early signals are stirring; watch whether more line up.

Score 0-2 CALM · 3-4 WATCH · 5-6 WARNING · 7-8 ALARM · 9-10 EXTREME (rare crash imminent). Lead times: structural 30-90d · stress 5-30d · triggers 1-10d. Four Telegram alerts available depending on escalation.
How to read
Each row = an independent signal Bonferroni-validated over 15 years of SPY. The structural (DXY, SKEW, MOVE) signal a regime change over 30-90 days. The stress (HYG/IEF, VIX3M/VIX, STLFSI4) ring at 5-30 days. The triggers (SPY -2% in 5d, VIX +30% in 3d) are short-term breakouts. For a tactical decision: 3+ signals simultaneously aligned = real probability of stress, never on isolated signal (framework rule).

Action — sizing tied to tier
Buy hedges B-tier (1-2% book) — VIX calls 30-60 DTE strike 25-28 OR SPY puts 60-90 DTE Δ0.20-0.30
How to read
4 tiers (BROAD / MEDIUM / HIGH / EXTREME) based on the confluence of 3 Validated components: SKEW > 140 (tail-hedge demand), VIX3M/VIX > 1.05 (vol curve inverted), VIX < 16 + ratio > 1.15 (adaptive proxy). Each tier indicates a historical probability of VIX +50% at 60d (cycle backtest 2023-2026, vs baseline 37%). Strict sizing: tier 2 = 1-2% book hedges, tier 3 = 3-5% long-vol, tier 4 = 5-10% MAX (rare confluence).

Aggregated bias · 53-ratio composite
Regime synthesis — RISK-ON / OFF / STRESS / TOPPING
QUIET — no extreme signals aligned Below the noise threshold. No Kerberos action zone.
RISK-ON aligned (3)
• Small caps leading
• Copper > Gold
• Industrials > Utilities
RISK-OFF aligned (0)
none
STRESS aligned (1)
• Dollar wrecking ball
TOPPING aligned (0)
none
How to read
Aggregation of all panel signals across the 4 cross-asset buckets. RISK-ON = risk appetite (cyclical, equity, EM). RISK-OFF = flight to defensives (TLT, gold, JPY). STRESS = liquidity tension (HYG, MOVE, STLFSI). TOPPING = end-of-cycle signals (concentration, breadth narrowing, defensives leading). Doctrine: a single signal = noise. 3+ aligned in one bucket = real confirmed regime.

EV% = win_rate × (target−entry) − (1−win_rate) × (entry−stop). Tier = T1 highest conviction. 52w% = % of 52-week high. Entry/stop/target levels live on the Trade Desk →
How to read
Heat ranking: composite of RS (relative strength vs SPY), Momentum (price action 20-60d), Breadth (% stocks above SMA), Options flow, and Attention. Use top-5 as rotation focus.
Emerging industries: high velocity = screener scores accelerating this week vs prior baseline — potential early rotation signal.
Competitor themes: token frequency × recency across top finance Substacks — useful narrative context, not a trading signal.
Best R/R: only entries with an active screener leg (PULLBACK / BOTTOM / SMART_MONEY_SURGE etc.). EV% = probabilistic edge per trade. Cross-check with weekly chart before sizing.

Reading the table — Z-Score: number of standard deviations vs rolling 100d mean. |Z| > 2 = Kerberos entry threshold, |Z| > 2.5 = extreme (best R/R on mean-reversion). %B: position within Bollinger Bands (1.0 = upper band, 0 = lower band).

Aggregate reading — the mean of Z-scores gives a global regime indicator. Positive → cross-asset overbought market (Risk-On, watch reversal). Negative → oversold (Risk-Off, possible bottoming).
How to read
Persist = number of consecutive days above |Z| > 2.0 (Kerberos threshold). Persist ≥ 3d = validated signal (the divergence has become a regime, not a spike). Persist 1-2d = to watch. Persist 0d = dead signal. Sort on this column to find established regimes worth trading.
Glossary — 18 terms to understand the dashboard
Z-Score
Number of standard deviations vs the historical mean (rolling 100 days). Z=0 = normal level · |Z|>2 = stress · |Z|>2.5 = extreme.
Bollinger Bands
Bands drawn at ±2.2σ around a 100-day moving average. When the price exits the bands = statistically extreme signal.
Backwardation
Inverted curve: immediate fear exceeds far fear (e.g. VIX > VIX3M). Short-term panic signal.
Contango
Normal situation where far fear exceeds immediate fear. Extreme contango = complacency.
Half-life
Number of days required for a gap to the mean to halve. Measures the speed of mean-reversion.
Cointegration
Two assets that move together in the long term despite short-term fluctuations. Statistical basis of pair-trading.
Mean-reversion
Strategy betting that a gap to the mean will close. Inverse of momentum (which bets on continuation).
Breadth
Width of participation in the rally. Good breadth = many stocks rising. Bad breadth = only a few mega-caps pull the index.
Hedge ratio
Quantity of asset B to short to neutralize 1 long unit of asset A. Calculated via OLS regression on historical returns.
Lead-time
Delay between a signal's appearance and the event it predicts. Structural = 30-90d · stress = 5-30d · triggers = 1-10d.
Bonferroni
Statistical correction for multiple testing. When testing 100 signals, some seem valid by chance. Bonferroni divides the significance threshold by the number of tests to eliminate these false positives.
MCPT (Monte Carlo Permutation Test)
Robustness test: data is randomly shuffled 5000-10000 times to see if the signal appears by chance. If p < 0.05, the signal is probably real.
Walk-forward
Test where the model is trained on the first years (e.g. 2010-2020) and tested on the following years (2020-2025). Avoids in-sample overfitting.
Tail-hedge
Hedge against an extreme crash (-20% or more). Bought via deeply out-of-the-money puts. SKEW measures institutional demand for this type of insurance.
Mega-caps
The 7-10 largest market caps in the SPX (AAPL, MSFT, NVDA, GOOGL, AMZN, META, TSLA). Weigh ~35% of the index.
Cap-weighted vs Equal-weighted
SPY = weighted by capitalization (mega-caps dominate). RSP = each stock weighs 0.2%. Comparing both reveals concentration.
Risk parity
Portfolio strategy that balances risk (not capital) across asset classes. Stressed when stocks/bonds correlations turn positive (inflation regime).
framework rule
Fundamental principle: a solo signal = noise, 3+ aligned signals = real edge. All panel signals are Bonferroni-validated over 15 years of SPY.
